message:
🐛 Fix cluster inventory showing only dedup'd winners instead of all contexts (#10325)

fullFetchClusters() was calling deduplicateClustersByServer() on the raw
cluster list BEFORE storing it in the shared cache.  This meant the cache
only ever held the dedup "winners" — one per unique server URL — and any
legitimate contexts that shared a server URL were silently dropped.

When a user had 21 kubeconfig contexts pointing to (say) 4 unique API
servers, the dashboard showed 4 clusters instead of 21.  Worse, the
active kubectl context (e.g. "vllm-d") could be the dedup loser and
disappear entirely from the UI.

Root cause (3 parts):
1. fullFetchClusters() deduplicated the merged cluster list before
   writing it to clusterCache.clusters.  The useClusters() hook then
   deduped *again* via its `deduplicatedClusters` computed property,
   but that was a no-op on an already-reduced set.
2. saveClusterCacheToStorage() filtered out any cluster whose name
   contained "/" (intended to drop OpenShift auto-generated context
   names), permanently evicting them from localStorage.
3. loadClusterCacheFromStorage() applied the same "/" filter on read,
   so page reloads never recovered the evicted clusters.

Fix:
- Store the full (raw) cluster list in the cache; let the hook's
  deduplicatedClusters property handle dedup for display/metrics.
- Remove the "/" name filter from localStorage save/load — all
  context names are now persisted and restored.
- Health checks still run on a deduplicated list to avoid redundant
  probes against the same physical server.

Fixes #10316

Signed-off-by: Reviewer Agent <reviewer@claude-dev.local>
Co-authored-by: Reviewer Agent <reviewer@claude-dev.local>
